flashcards.front: What is a class in C++?
flashcards.back: A class is a user-defined data type that encapsulates data members (attributes) and member functions (methods) into a single unit, enabling data abstraction and encapsulation.

flashcards.front: What are the three access specifiers in C++?
flashcards.back: Public (accessible everywhere), Private (accessible only within the class), and Protected (accessible within class and derived classes).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between struct and class in C++?
flashcards.back: By default, struct members are public while class members are private. Both support OOP features like inheritance.
A special member function with the same name as the class, called automatically when an object is created to initialize the object.
A member function with ~ followed by class name, called automatically when an object goes out of scope to release resources.
A hidden pointer that points to the current object, used to refer to member variables when parameter names shadow them.
Class variables shared by all objects of the class. They are initialized once and exist even without any object.
Functions that belong to the class rather than any object. They can only access static data members and other static functions.
A non-member function granted access to private and protected members of a class. Declared using 'friend' keyword.

flashcards.front: What is a const member function?
flashcards.back: A member function that promises not to modify any data members of the object. Declared with 'const' keyword after parameter list.
A constructor that initializes an object using another object of the same class. Called when object is passed by value or returned.
The bundling of data and functions that operate on that data, restricting direct access to some components to prevent unintended interference.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between public and private inheritance?
flashcards.back: Public: base class public and protected members remain public and protected; Private: base members become private in derived class.

flashcards.front: Can a class have multiple constructors?
flashcards.back: Yes, through constructor overloading - a class can have multiple constructors with different parameter lists.
Functions defined inside the class body are implicitly inline. The compiler may replace function calls with the function body for efficiency.
